steps.title: Find each object's speed
steps.content: Object R covers $4x$ inches in $y$ seconds, so its speed is $\frac{4x}{y}$ inches per second. Object S is twice as fast: $2 \cdot \frac{4x}{y} = \frac{8x}{y}$ inches per second.

steps.title: Compute time for object S to travel 24x
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) $12y$ — this would be the time at speed $\frac{2x}{y}$, half of R's speed instead of double. Wrong.
  • B) $3y$ — matches $\frac{24x}{8x/y} = 3y$. Correct.
  • C) $16y$ — far too large; corresponds to no consistent speed ratio. Wrong.
  • D) $6y$ — this is the time R would take to travel $24x$ (since R needs $y$ per $4x$, so $6y$ per $24x$), not S. Wrong.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
